physiology.md: The Physiology topic defines two metabolic boundaries that organize all endurance training: the Aerobic Threshold (AeT/VT1/LT1) at roughly 2 mmol/L lactate and the Anaerobic Threshold (AnT/VT2/LT2/OBLA) where lactate production outpaces clearance.
Below the Aerobic Threshold fat is the primary fuel (about 100,000 stored calories available) and exercise can theoretically continue indefinitely.
The Anaerobic Threshold represents the highest sustainable effort for approximately 30 to 60 minutes.
The Lactate Shuttle, discovered by George Brooks in the 1980s, describes how slow-twitch fibers absorb and re-oxidize lactate produced by fast-twitch fibers.
A wide AeT-AnT gap indicates insufficient lactate-clearing capacity in the aerobic base.
Mitochondrial biogenesis, one of the four pillars of aerobic adaptation, is triggered by the AMPK pathway under prolonged low-intensity exercise.
Excessive lactate from high-intensity training downregulates the AMPK pathway and undermines aerobic development.
Fat oxidation adaptation shifts the crossover point to higher intensities and is critical for events lasting 8 to 16+ hours.
Capillary density adaptation improves oxygen delivery and waste removal and is especially important at altitude.
Muscle fiber plasticity allows Type II fibers to develop oxidative capacity equal to Type I with years of consistent aerobic work.
Aerobic Deficiency Syndrome is defined as an AeT-AnT gap greater than 10% and affects 80-90% of athletes.
Aerobic Deficiency Syndrome is reversible with months of sub-AeT training, with documented improvements of 25-30 bpm in AeT heart rate.
Endurance performance rests on three determinants agreed by both Uphill Athlete and Daniels: VO2max, running economy, and lactate threshold.
VO2max has a genetic ceiling but is trainable, especially in non-trained athletes, via Daniels I-pace interval work at 95-100% VO2max with 3-5 minute repeats.
VO2 kinetics require about 2 minutes of running at I pace to reach VO2max, so shorter intervals waste ramp-up time.
Running economy is trainable via Daniels R-pace repetition work at mile pace or faster with repeats under 2 minutes, improving neuromuscular coordination and biomechanical efficiency.
Running economy is also improved by stride-rate optimization targeting 180+ spm and by accumulated mileage.
Lactate threshold is the most trainable and impactful of the three performance determinants, per both Uphill Athlete and Daniels.
Daniels T-pace threshold work (20-minute tempo runs or 3-15 minute cruise intervals with 1 minute recovery) develops lactate threshold.
Daniels T pace corresponds to approximately 83-88% of VO2max and is sustainable for about 50-60 minutes in race conditions.
VDOT is Daniels' integrative metric: a single race-derived number reflecting the net effect of VO2max, running economy, and lactate threshold on performance, eliminating the need for lab testing.
The Frontier Fiber Model holds that each increase in running speed recruits faster-twitch frontier fibers whose local endurance limits performance at that speed.
The Central Governor Theory posits that the brain regulates fatigue through a self-preservation mechanism that limits muscle activation as fatigue develops.

The Fitzgerald/Marcora psychobiological model extends the Central Governor Theory by arguing that conscious perception of effort, not unconscious brain regulation, is the primary limiter of endurance.
Glycogen depletion from long sessions is described as one of the most powerful training stimuli for aerobic adaptation, triggering mitochondrial biogenesis via the AMPK pathway.

Seiler observed that elite endurance athletes train 80-85% of volume at low intensity, and Stoggl & Sperlich (2014) reported +11.7% VO2peak and +17.4% time-to-exhaustion improvements on polarized training programs.
Daniels emphasizes that easy E-pace training drives cardiovascular adaptations including increased stroke volume, improved cardiac output, and enhanced oxygen delivery.
Women have smaller hearts and lungs and a lower VO2max ceiling, which affects high-intensity performance more than endurance performance.
Women have less type II muscle fiber and lower glycogen storage capacity, giving men an advantage in short intense bursts and women greater fatigue resistance in endurance.
Women rely more on fat for fuel during aerobic exercise, giving them an endurance advantage at sub-threshold intensities.
Women sweat less, start sweating at higher core temperatures, and produce more diluted sweat, and progesterone in the luteal phase further impairs heat dissipation.
Heat acclimation for female athletes requires 14-21 days, and pre-cooling is especially important for women in hot conditions.
The menstrual cycle creates a roughly 28-day oscillation in estrogen and progesterone that affects muscle protein synthesis, blood sugar regulation, core temperature, fluid balance, and recovery.
The low-hormone follicular phase is more favorable for high-intensity work and strength gains, while the high-hormone luteal phase impairs these processes.
Peak female bone mass occurs at age 30-35 and declines thereafter, accelerating sharply during menopause with estrogen loss.
Postmenopausal women can gain roughly 1% bone density per year in spine and hips with appropriate impact exercise (running, jumping, strength training).
Women lose about 3% muscle mass per decade after age 30 without strength training, faster than men due to hormonal differences.
Menopause triggers body composition shifts (fat accumulation, muscle loss), increased carbohydrate sensitivity, decreased fructose tolerance, sleep disturbances, and thermoregulation changes.
Stacy Sims' core thesis 'women are not small men' argues that training and nutrition protocols derived from male-subject research are often suboptimal for women.
run-sync.js: The run-sync.js connector is a Node.js script that pulls data from connected services and writes a structured snapshot to state/sync_YYYY-MM-DD.md.
run-sync.js supports CLI flags --since <date> to sync from a specific date and --json to output raw JSON instead of the markdown snapshot.
By default run-sync.js syncs the last 7 days, computing the since date as now minus 7*86400000 milliseconds when --since is not passed.
run-sync.js loads environment variables from a .env file at the project root by parsing KEY=value lines and assigning them into process.env.
run-sync.js imports sibling connector modules ./strava, ./google-calendar, and ./token-store to delegate service-specific work.
run-sync.js only runs the Strava branch when loadTokens('strava') returns tokens and STRAVA_CLIENT_ID is set in the environment.
For Strava, run-sync.js refreshes the access token via strava.refreshAccessToken using STRAVA_CLIENT_ID and STRAVA_CLIENT_SECRET before fetching activities.
run-sync.js fetches Strava activities with strava.getAllActivities using the since date as 'after' and an until timestamp built as until + T23:59:59 as 'before'.
run-sync.js summarizes Strava activities by piping raw data through strava.formatActivities and strava.summarizeActivities into results.strava.
run-sync.js only runs the Google Calendar branch when loadTokens('google') returns tokens and GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is set in the environment.
For Google Calendar, run-sync.js looks forward rather than backward, setting timeMin to now and timeMax to now + 10 days because upcoming conflicts matter more than past events.
run-sync.js summarizes Google Calendar events via google.formatEvents and google.summarizeWeek into results.google.
Per-connector errors in run-sync.js are caught, logged to stderr, and stored as { error: err.message } on the corresponding results field rather than aborting the whole sync.
When --json is passed, run-sync.js prints the raw results JSON to stdout and skips markdown snapshot generation entirely.
run-sync.js writes a markdown snapshot to <project>/state/sync_<until>.md, creating the state directory recursively if it does not exist.
The buildMarkdown helper in run-sync.js renders the Strava section as a pipe-delimited activity table followed by totals and the Google Calendar section as a per-day bulleted event list.
run-sync.js main().catch handler prints 'Sync failed: <message>' to stderr and exits with code 1 on uncaught failures.

subagent_report.subagent_count_discrepancy: Subagent reported 38+18=56 but raw dump shows 39+17=56 — one claim miscategorized in the verbal report, raw data is authoritative.

prose_feedback.redundant: addressing node restates entity reuse guidance already implied in staging
prose_feedback.unclear: onEnter memory_browse hook error when collection not pre-registered in config.yml — error surfaces from hook, not from freelance_start, and message gives no hint that collection must be declared

subagent_report.config_edit: subagent added ablation-1-beta to .freelance/config.yml memory.collections to unblock the onEnter memory_browse hook
